text: I am Dinesh Kansal from Delhi, India. We have a right and true lateral single pan-mural fibroid, six point seven centimeter size for my machine. Da Vinci XI robot.

The surgeon inserts the primary port blindly, followed by the insertion of additional ports under direct vision. A midline port is used for the camera, with one port on the left and two on the right, designated as the third and fourth arms. An assistant port is positioned slightly above, and docking is completed using a port clutch. All ports are 8 millimeters in diameter, and the telescope is inserted.

The robotic arms are attached to the ports, and instruments are inserted into the surgical field. The surgeon confirms the correct positioning of the ports using a visible marker. The assistant manages the insertion and removal of instruments as needed for cleaning or changing.
I go to the surgeon's console to start the surgery. The surgeon has a wonderful high-definition,
The bedside assistant injects vasopressin into the fibroid, causing visible blanching of the tissue.

The surgeon makes a vertical incision on the fibroid using monopolar scissors, known as hot shears. The incision is visible on the screen with indicators showing the position of the third arm on the robotic system.

The surgeon activates cutting or coagulating energy to reach the correct plane of the fibroid, using a fenestrated bipolar grasper in the left hand. The fibroid is visibly emerging from the incision, aided by the high-definition vision of the surgical console.
The surgeon performs blunt dissection in the correct plane using robotic arms. The fourth arm is activated for retraction, while the third and first arms are engaged in active surgical maneuvers.

The surgeon reaches the submucosal part of the fibroid, where tiny feeding vessels are visible. Slight energy is applied to these vascular connections, avoiding the myometrium, while manipulating the fibroid using surgical arms.

Usually the bleeding is lesser in robotic surgery compared to laparoscopic or open because the smaller vessels are also seen very well in this. And as the movements are at the rest of the instrument, the movements at the port are very less and hence there is less stretching of the abdominal wall and hence these patients hardly complain of any pain post-operatively. As you can see the movements are the three sixty degree movements of the wrist of the instrument.
The fibroid is nearly extracted from the myoma bed and is set aside. The surgeon begins suturing the area, successfully avoiding opening the endometrium.

The movement of the fingers is downsized as it goes to the tip of the instrument. Camera control is in surgeon's hand. When we control the camera, the instruments freeze and vice versa. We can toggle with our left foot. For the right, both the arms. And energy is in control with the right foot. If we press the bipolar, then the monopolar is not activated because the foot will be kept either on the monopolar pedal or bipolar pedal. So hence, precision and safety is very well managed in robotic surgery.
The surgeon prepares to close the U-triangle using a bark suture technique, employing a V-lock suture. The needle is visibly passed through the loop to secure the first layer of closure.

The surgeon tightens the suture, achieving a precise approximation of the tissues to close the dead space. The first layer of suturing is completed on the deep and large fibroid, with visible wrist movements enhancing precision during the robotic procedure.

The surgeon completes the first layer of suturing and begins the second layer using a continuous suture technique. The robotic instruments facilitate precise inside-out and outside-in base wall sutures, minimizing myometrium exposure and reducing the risk of adhesion formation in the peritoneal cavity.

The surgeon completes the first suture and cuts the thread. The bedside assistant removes the needle and provides a new suture for the surgeon to begin the second suture, continuing in a continuous fashion.
The surgeon completes the suturing process. Bipolar coagulation is applied to the right fallopian tube, followed by cutting with scissors. The same procedure is performed on the left fallopian tube.
The screen displays activity on either side, corresponding to the energized surgical instruments. The specimen is received through morcellation, visibly fragmented for removal.

These patients had a wonderful recovery. She was discharged the next day and on the fifth day, we removed her staples and she went to her own country. She was an international patient. Just small two bits. We will carefully take them out. The patient after fifth day was very happy when she went home pain-free. Thank you.
